subject: Chemistry
title: Fukushima: When the Sea Struck Back
description: One-minute skill: Fukushima: When the Sea Struck Back.

hook.text: In 2011, a 14-meter tsunami overwhelmed Fukushima's seawalls, flooding the backup generators and causing three reactor meltdowns.

buildup.text: The earthquake shut down the reactors automatically. But without cooling pumps, residual heat kept building in the fuel.

discovery.text: Zirconium fuel cladding reacted with steam at high temperature, producing hydrogen gas that exploded in three reactor buildings.

twist.text: The plant's seawalls were designed for 5.7-meter waves. The 2011 tsunami was 14 meters—the risk analysis was fatally wrong.

climax.text: Over a million tons of treated radioactive water have been gradually released into the Pacific since 2023.

punchline.text: Backup generators below sea level. The safety system failed to account for nature's worst.

quiz.question: What chemical reaction produced the hydrogen explosions at Fukushima?
options: Zirconium fuel cladding reacted with steam at high temperature
Uranium fuel rods split water molecules directly
Cooling water electrolyzed due to high radiation levels
